rxn_class_summary: Asymmetric electrocatalytic cyclopropanation of alpha,beta-unsaturated aldehydes with malonates via iminium ion organocatalysis and anodically generated electrophilic iodine
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide (Et4NI) to electrophilic iodine species (I+/I2) which iodinates the enamine intermediate; cathode reduces protons (from HFIP) to H2 as counter half-reaction

key_intermediates.name: iminium ion from cinnamaldehyde and diarylprolinol-TMS catalyst I (i)
enamine after Michael addition of malonate to iminium (ii)
Michael adduct aldehyde 4a (hydrolysis product of enamine ii)
electrophilic iodine species (I+ / I2) generated at anode

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes iodide (2 e-) to an electrophilic I+ species; the enamine (formed by Michael addition of malonate onto the chiral iminium) is iodinated to give an alpha-iodo iminium that undergoes intramolecular malonate alkylation (ring closure) releasing I-; hydrolysis of the resulting iminium gives the cyclopropane and regenerates the aminocatalyst. Cathode reduces protons (from HFIP) to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Only minor suppression of 3a (36% yield); TEMPO-adduct 6 observed (25% NMR yield); ambiguous since TEMPO can be oxidized to N-oxoammonium and react in polar fashion

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Cyclopropane 3a still formed (38% NMR), only 4% of 4a; no quenching of putative enamine radical cation -> argues for polar pathway

probe_reagents.name: pre-synthesized Michael adduct 4a
probe_reagents.observation: Under standard electrolysis 4a gave 43% 3a + 17% 5a; without aminocatalyst 4a gave 23% 3a (94% ee) confirming ring closure can proceed via enamine/iodination pathway

probe_reagents.name: molecular I2 / NIS (no electricity)
probe_reagents.observation: No conversion and catalyst I decomposed, showing that controlled electrocatalytic generation of I+ is essential to protect the aminocatalyst from Grob fragmentation

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of components
probe_reagents.observation: Iodide oxidizes at +0.19 and +0.38 V; enamine (II from 4a + cat I) oxidizes at +0.27 V; iodide is the lowest-potential and preferred species oxidized at the anode

notes: Authors favor a polar (iminium/enamine + electrophilic iodination) mechanism over a SET/radical cation pathway; an alternative enamine radical-cation route was considered (since enamine Eox = +0.27 V is below iodide's second oxidation) but BHT trapping and ring closure from isolated 4a without catalyst support the polar pathway. A radical pathway via TEMPO oxidation to N-oxoammonium adding to the enamine is mentioned in ESI Scheme S2. Side product 5a arises from base-induced retro-Michael of iminium iv.
